q: An object that moves in a circle at constant speed $v$ is said to experience ___ circular motion.
In uniform circular motion, the ___ of the velocity remains constant.
The change in ___ of velocity constitutes an acceleration in circular motion.
Acceleration $\vec{a}$ in uniform circular motion points toward the ___ of the circle.
Centripetal acceleration is also called ___ acceleration because it is directed along the radius.
The centripetal acceleration is denoted by the symbol ___.
The equation for centripetal acceleration in terms of linear speed is $a_c = ___$.
Substituting $v = r \omega$ into the centripetal acceleration equation gives $a_c = ___$.
In uniform circular motion, the centripetal acceleration and linear velocity are ___ to each other.
In ___ circular motion, an object moves in a circular path with a varying speed.
Nonuniform circular motion gives rise to ___ acceleration in addition to centripetal acceleration.
The resultant acceleration in nonuniform circular motion is the ___ sum of centripetal and tangential accelerations.
The magnitude of resultant acceleration is $a = \sqrt{a_c^2 + ___}$.
The direction of resultant acceleration is given by $\tan \phi = ___$.
Unlike tangential acceleration, ___ acceleration is present in both uniform and nonuniform circular motion.
